tickets.title: vault-critic AG hang — network theory REFUTED by 6/04 probe; hang is INSIDE gemini's request path, not the network. The 6/04 03:30 run fired the IP-family probe AT the 120s-timeout moment: BOTH Google hosts (cloudcode-pa + play.googleapis) connected in ~15-26ms on IPv4 AND IPv6, and the api.openai.com control connected too — i.e. the network was fully up while gemini's authenticated model request hung to 120s and returned 0 tokens. The 6/03 play.googleapis.com connect-timeouts were a red herring (telemetry endpoint, intermittent; the actual model host cloudcode-pa was reachable). So eliminated now: MCP, OAuth-refresh, machine-asleep, AND raw network reachability. What remains is the authenticated Code Assist streaming call silently hanging (server-side throttle-as-hang on the preview model, or a CLI stream bug) — but note: curl-reachable ≠ authed-call-works, and the CLI swallows the real error, so further --debug captures won't reveal more. RECOMMENDATION: stop chasing the gemini hang — the circuit breaker now contains it ($0 impact) and the June-18 oauth EOL obsoletes the whole path. Collapse this into the migration decision below
tickets.headline: vault-critic AG hang — network theory REFUTED by 6/04 probe; hang is INSIDE gemini's request path, not the network. The 6/03 play.googleapis.com connect-timeouts were a red herring (telemetry endpoint, intermittent; the actual model host cloudcode-pa was reachable). So eliminated now: MCP, OAuth-refresh, machine-asleep, AND raw network reachability. What remains is the authenticated Code Assist streaming call silently hanging (server-side throttle-as-hang on the preview model, or a CLI stream bug) — but note: curl-reachable ≠ authed-call-works, and the CLI swallows the real error, so further --debug captures won't reveal more. RECOMMENDATION: stop chasing the gemini hang — the circuit breaker now contains it ($0 impact) and the June-18 oauth EOL obsoletes the whole path. Collapse this into the migration decision below

tickets.title: vault-critic AG — DECISION (6/04, Sean): Codex-only for now (`antigravity_enabled = false` shipped); no Gemini API spend. POST-JUNE-18 TASK: re-enable AG via the Antigravity CLI (uses the paid Google-One subscription, NOT the metered API) and A/B test whether the second opinion is worth keeping — flip `antigravity_enabled = true` (or run once with `--antigravity`), watch a few nightlies, compare expansion quality vs Codex-only + duration. If the Antigravity CLI hits the same silent hang, add a hard <120s stream timeout or drop AG for good. gemini-cli oauth-personal EOL is 6/18 (flag 45740200) — don't test the old gemini-cli path after that, go straight to Antigravity CLI
tickets.headline: vault-critic AG — DECISION (6/04, Sean): Codex-only for now (`antigravity_enabled = false` shipped); no Gemini API spend. If the Antigravity CLI hits the same silent hang, add a hard <120s stream timeout or drop AG for good. gemini-cli oauth-personal EOL is 6/18 (flag 45740200) — don't test the old gemini-cli path after that, go straight to Antigravity CLI

tickets.title: vault-critic budget waste — RESOLVED + VERIFIED on the 6/04 nightly. AG circuit-breaker tripped after 1 failure exactly as designed: manifest shows antigravity_calls=1, antigravity_skipped=7, codex_calls=8, articles=3 fresh + 5 backfill = 8 (was 5 on 6/03), codex_tokens=260K (was 145K), duration=323s (was 610s). Throughput +60%, budget headroom restored. With ~277s spare now, could raise backfill_max_per_run (currently 5) to drain the orphan backlog faster. Raise threshold to 2 (or 0=off) once AG is healthy/migrated

tickets.title: Obsidian-Git stale `.git/index.lock` recurs (fixed 5/31 and 6/03) — root cause is git commit crashing mid-write when Modern Standby cuts power during the 60-min auto-commit cycle. Permanent fix instead of repeated manual `rm .git/index.lock`: either widen the commit interval, gate commits on machine-awake, or add a pre-commit stale-lock sweep that only deletes a >5-min-old EMPTY index.lock with no live git process
tickets.headline: Obsidian-Git stale `.git/index.lock` recurs (fixed 5/31 and 6/03) — root cause is git commit crashing mid-write when Modern Standby cuts power during the 60-min auto-commit cycle. Permanent fix instead of repeated manual `rm .git/index.lock`: either widen the commit interval, gate commits on machine-awake, or add a pre-commit stale-lock sweep that only deletes a >5-min-old EMPTY index.lock with no live git process

Cy's Pass-3 now routes through the Gemini API transport (gemini-3.5-flash, pinned by ID, served model read back from resp.model_version) per the A2 hardening PR; this ticket is the COSTED re-run of the two locked Bibles on that pinned path, scheduled AFTER the hardening PR merges. tickets.title: ESCALATION (2026-06-03, B1b DINOv2 spike): this is BIGGER than "Flash-verified." sean-anchor's BODY turnarounds are proportion-OFF — head reads ~1:5.3 (profile-right), ~1:4.6 (back), ~1:4-4.5 (walk-cycle) instead of the 1:7 target — and were baked into the LOCKED Bible anyway because the Pass-2.5 similarity gate is record-only (it never measured proportion). So a re-verify on a pinned model isn't enough; the body plates need a proportion re-bake to clean 1:7, then re-lock. Evidence: anima PR #20 + evals/vision_critic/traces/dinov2-separation-2026-06-03.md.

tickets.title: writing-voice-modes eval re-baseline — RUN ON YOUR MAC (prep DONE 2026-06-06; harness can't run in Cowork). Generation now routes through your Claude SUBSCRIPTION via the Agent SDK (OAuth), NOT the Anthropic API key — _SubscriptionClient passes env={} (never the key) and warns if one is set. Prereqs: `claude login` valid, NO ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in env (unset it to be safe), Mac Mini Ollama up at localhost:5050 with qwen3-14b-research. Steps already done by Claude: stylometry baseline rebuilt (em-dash 1.41→0.08, de-contaminated) + threshold recomputed offline (=9.64, reuse-existing, zero API). FIRST RUN FAILED 2026-06-07 (SDK exit-1, swallowed) — root-caused to the invalid model string `claude-opus-4-7`; FIXED 2026-06-07 (switched to the `opus`/`sonnet` Agent SDK aliases, dropped empty-valued `--setting-sources=`/`--tools` flags, wired the stderr callback so any next failure prints `[claude-cli] ...`). RE-RUN now on the Mac: `cd agents-sdk && PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m agents.skill_optimizer --score-only` (5 train + 2 holdout × 15 = 105 Opus gens via subscription + local Qwen judge; writes a score-only-<date> row). If it still errors, the stderr callback will now show the real CLI reason (likely model-availability on the plan, or Ollama down) — paste it back. Compare train_score to the 2026-05-10 baseline 0.7267 — but read it knowing the eval is blunt (see next ticket). If you ever re-run the FULL calibration with fresh AI samples, that path still needs an API key (`calibrate_stylometry_threshold.py` without --reuse-existing) — prefer `--reuse-existing` to stay key-free

tickets.title: writing-voice-modes eval is TOO BLUNT to detect the grit/90-10 overhaul: `sounds_like_sean` was pinned at 1.00 in the only prior run, so the criterion that should register a voice improvement has no headroom; the criteria with headroom are format-mechanics + the (now-fixed) stylometry signal. Redesign the eval to discriminate: make sounds_like_sean harder (graded/sub-checks), add a cheese-bank NEGATIVE criterion (reject MEANING_OVER_ACCESS-register output), add a grit-register check. Until then a re-baseline number won't tell you if the overhaul worked
